---
title: Visualizing UBC subclusters
date: 2024-06-26
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
---

# Goal of this analysis

1. Create a DotPlot for UBC subclusters + control cells and look at expression
2. Create a HeatMap with the 5000 most variable genes. 
    a. One with the `data` slot
    b. One with the `scale.data` slot

# Files and directories

## Input

CCA RL file is found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240618/rl_cca.qs`

## Output

The output can be found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240626`

# Process and Results

Using the `scrnaseq_env` conda environment

Lets load in the required libraries
```{r eval=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(Seurat)
library(qs)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(patchwork)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)
```

Loading in the Seurat object
```{r eval=FALSE}
out_directory <- "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240626"
rl_cca <- qread("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240618/rl_cca.qs")
```

Creating the DotPlot
```{r eval=FALSE}
# Genes of interest
genes <- read.csv("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/scrnaseq_Leo/utilities/cell_gene_mapping.csv")
genes <- genes$gene

# Dot plot
dot_plot <- DotPlot(object = rl_cca, features = genes, assay = "RNA", group.by = "broad_w_ubc_subtypes") + theme_classic()
ggsave(filename = "dot_plot.png", plot = dot_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 25, height = 5, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

Finding the common genes between datasets
```{r eval=FALSE}
DefaultAssay(rl_cca) <- "RNA"

ald <- subset(rl_cca, subset = dataset_name == "Aldinger_full_cerebellum_human")
sepp <- subset(rl_cca, subset = dataset_name == "Sepp_full_cerebellum_human")

counts_ald <- GetAssayData(object = ald, slot = "counts")
nonzero_ald <- counts_ald > 0
keep_ald_genes <- Matrix::rowSums(nonzero_ald) >= 10
ald_genes <- rownames(ald)[keep_ald_genes]

counts_sepp <- GetAssayData(object = sepp, slot = "counts")
nonzero_sepp <- counts_sepp > 0
keep_sepp_genes <- Matrix::rowSums(nonzero_sepp) >= 10
sepp_genes <- rownames(sepp)[keep_sepp_genes]

intersecting_genes <- intersect(ald_genes, sepp_genes)  
```

Creating the Heatmap
```{r eval=FALSE}
# First subset human cells, then subset for UBC cells, then normalize, find the variable features, and scale data
ubc_cells <- rl_cca %>%
  subset(features = intersecting_genes) %>%
  subset(subset = species == "human") %>%
  subset(subset = broad_cell_type == "UBC") %>%
  NormalizeData(assay = "RNA") %>%
  FindVariableFeatures(nfeatures = 5000, assay = "RNA") %>%
  ScaleData(vars.to.regress = "CC.Difference", assay = "RNA")

# Randomly sample 100 cell names
#set.seed(1)
#sampled_cells <- sample(colnames(ubc_cells), 50)
#sampled_ubc_cells <- subset(ubc_cells, cells = sampled_cells)

# Extract the RNA assay data from the data slot and convert to matrix
ubc_data <- GetAssayData(ubc_cells, assay = "RNA", slot = "data")
ubc_data_matrix <- ubc_data[VariableFeatures(ubc_cells), ] %>%
    as.matrix()
#ubc_data <- GetAssayData(sampled_ubc_cells, assay = "RNA", slot = "data")
#ubc_data_matrix <- ubc_data[VariableFeatures(sampled_ubc_cells), ] %>%
    #as.matrix()

# Extract the metadata
columns <- c("new_clusters","dataset_name")
ubc_metadata <- ubc_cells@meta.data[, columns]
#ubc_metadata <- sampled_ubc_cells@meta.data[, columns]

ubc_metadata$new_clusters <- factor(ubc_metadata$new_clusters, levels = unique(ubc_metadata$new_clusters))
ubc_metadata$dataset_name <- factor(ubc_metadata$dataset_name, levels = unique(ubc_metadata$dataset_name))

# Remove genes that although are variable have 0 expression within the cells
  # Mainly for when randomly sampling
ubc_data_matrix <- ubc_data_matrix[rowSums(ubc_data_matrix != 0) > 0, ]
```
```{r eval=FALSE}
png(file.path(out_directory, "ubc_data_heatmap.png"),width=20,height=20,units="in",res=600)

# Setting colour pallette
cluster_palette <- brewer.pal(length(levels(ubc_metadata$new_clusters)), "Dark2")
names(cluster_palette) <- levels(ubc_metadata$new_clusters)
#dataset_palette <- brewer.pal(length(levels(ubc_metadata$dataset_name)), "Set2")
dataset_palette <- c("#66C2A5", "#FC8D62")
names(dataset_palette) <- levels(ubc_metadata$dataset_name)

# Column annotation
column_annotation <- HeatmapAnnotation(
  Group = ubc_metadata$new_clusters,
  Dataset = ubc_metadata$dataset_name,
  col = list(
    Group = cluster_palette,
    Dataset = dataset_palette
  ),
  show_legend = c(
    "Group" = FALSE,  
    "Dataset" = FALSE  
  ),
  show_annotation_name = FALSE
)

cluster_legend <- Legend(labels = names(cluster_palette), 
    title = "UBC Subclusters", 
    title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
    title_position = "leftcenter-rot",
    grid_width = unit(0.5, "in"),
    grid_height = unit(1, "in"),
    legend_gp = gpar(fill = cluster_palette))

dataset_legend <- Legend(labels = names(dataset_palette), 
    title = "Datasets", 
    title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
    title_position = "leftcenter-rot",
    grid_width = unit(0.5, "in"),
    grid_height = unit(1, "in"),
    legend_gp = gpar(fill = dataset_palette))

# Heatmap
heatmap_col <- brewer.pal(4, "Blues")
col_fun = colorRamp2(c(0, 1, 2, 3), heatmap_col)

heatmap <- Heatmap(ubc_data_matrix,
    col = col_fun,
    top_annotation = column_annotation,
    width = unit(15, "in"), height = unit(15, "in"),
    row_title = "Variable Genes", row_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 20, fontface = "bold"),
    column_title = "UBC Cells", column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 20, fontface = "bold"),
    row_dend_reorder = TRUE,
    show_row_names = FALSE,  
    show_column_names = FALSE,  
    heatmap_legend_param = list(
        title = "Gene Expression",
        title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
        title_position = "leftcenter-rot",
        grid_width = unit(0.5, "in"),
        legend_height = unit(6, "in")))

draw(heatmap, 
     heatmap_legend_side = "right", 
     annotation_legend_side = "right", 
     annotation_legend_list = list(cluster_legend, dataset_legend))

dev.off()
```

![It appears that the UBCs are in 2 major clusters, the datasets. I think I need to filter the genes a lot better.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240626/ubc_data_heatmap.png)

Lets try some filters. I only want genes that are expressed more widely in the UBC subsets for both datasets.
```{r eval=FALSE}
rl_cca <- qread("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240618/rl_cca.qs")
ubc_cells <- rl_cca %>%
  subset(subset = species == "human") %>%
  subset(subset = broad_cell_type == "UBC")
```

Finding the common genes between datasets
```{r eval=FALSE}
DefaultAssay(ubc_cells) <- "RNA"

ald <- subset(ubc_cells, subset = dataset_name == "Aldinger_full_cerebellum_human")
sepp <- subset(ubc_cells, subset = dataset_name == "Sepp_full_cerebellum_human")

counts_ald <- GetAssayData(object = ald, slot = "counts")
nonzero_ald <- counts_ald > 0
keep_ald_genes <- Matrix::rowSums(nonzero_ald) >= 150
ald_genes <- rownames(ald)[keep_ald_genes]

counts_sepp <- GetAssayData(object = sepp, slot = "counts")
nonzero_sepp <- counts_sepp > 0
keep_sepp_genes <- Matrix::rowSums(nonzero_sepp) >= 150
sepp_genes <- rownames(sepp)[keep_sepp_genes]

intersecting_genes <- intersect(ald_genes, sepp_genes)  
```
```{r eval=FALSE}
> print(length(ald_genes))
[1] 3691
> print(length(sepp_genes))
[1] 4291
> print(length(intersecting_genes))
[1] 3515
```
```{r eval=FALSE}
# First subset human cells, then subset for UBC cells, then normalize, find the variable features, and scale data
ubc_cells <- ubc_cells %>%
  subset(features = intersecting_genes) %>%
  NormalizeData(assay = "RNA") %>%
  FindVariableFeatures(nfeatures = 2000, assay = "RNA") %>%
  ScaleData(vars.to.regress = "CC.Difference", assay = "RNA")

# Randomly sample 100 cell names
#set.seed(1)
#sampled_cells <- sample(colnames(ubc_cells), 100)
#sampled_ubc_cells <- subset(ubc_cells, cells = sampled_cells)

# Extract the RNA assay data from the data slot and convert to matrix
ubc_data <- GetAssayData(ubc_cells, assay = "RNA", slot = "data")
ubc_data_matrix <- ubc_data[VariableFeatures(ubc_cells), ] %>%
    as.matrix()
#ubc_data <- GetAssayData(sampled_ubc_cells, assay = "RNA", slot = "data")
#ubc_data_matrix <- ubc_data[VariableFeatures(sampled_ubc_cells), ] %>%
    #as.matrix()

# Extract the metadata
columns <- c("new_clusters","dataset_name")
ubc_metadata <- ubc_cells@meta.data[, columns]
#ubc_metadata <- sampled_ubc_cells@meta.data[, columns]

ubc_metadata$new_clusters <- factor(ubc_metadata$new_clusters, levels = unique(ubc_metadata$new_clusters))
ubc_metadata$dataset_name <- factor(ubc_metadata$dataset_name, levels = unique(ubc_metadata$dataset_name))

# Remove genes that although are variable have 0 expression within the cells
  # Mainly for when randomly sampling
ubc_data_matrix <- ubc_data_matrix[rowSums(ubc_data_matrix != 0) > 0, ]
```
```{r eval=FALSE}
png(file.path(out_directory, "filtered_ubc_data_heatmap.png"),width=20,height=20,units="in",res=600)

# Setting colour pallette
cluster_palette <- brewer.pal(length(levels(ubc_metadata$new_clusters)), "Dark2")
names(cluster_palette) <- levels(ubc_metadata$new_clusters)
#dataset_palette <- brewer.pal(length(levels(ubc_metadata$dataset_name)), "Set2")
dataset_palette <- c("#66C2A5", "#FC8D62")
names(dataset_palette) <- levels(ubc_metadata$dataset_name)

# Column annotation
column_annotation <- HeatmapAnnotation(
  Group = ubc_metadata$new_clusters,
  Dataset = ubc_metadata$dataset_name,
  col = list(
    Group = cluster_palette,
    Dataset = dataset_palette
  ),
  show_legend = c(
    "Group" = FALSE,  
    "Dataset" = FALSE  
  ),
  show_annotation_name = FALSE
)

cluster_legend <- Legend(labels = names(cluster_palette), 
    title = "UBC Subclusters", 
    title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
    title_position = "leftcenter-rot",
    grid_width = unit(0.5, "in"),
    grid_height = unit(1, "in"),
    legend_gp = gpar(fill = cluster_palette))

dataset_legend <- Legend(labels = names(dataset_palette), 
    title = "Datasets", 
    title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
    title_position = "leftcenter-rot",
    grid_width = unit(0.5, "in"),
    grid_height = unit(1, "in"),
    legend_gp = gpar(fill = dataset_palette))

# Heatmap
heatmap_col <- brewer.pal(4, "Blues")
col_fun = colorRamp2(c(0, 1, 2, 3), heatmap_col)

heatmap <- Heatmap(ubc_data_matrix,
    col = col_fun,
    top_annotation = column_annotation,
    width = unit(15, "in"), height = unit(15, "in"),
    row_title = "Variable Genes", row_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 20, fontface = "bold"),
    column_title = "UBC Cells", column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 20, fontface = "bold"),
    row_dend_reorder = TRUE,
    show_row_names = FALSE,  
    show_column_names = FALSE,  
    heatmap_legend_param = list(
        title = "Gene Expression",
        title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
        title_position = "leftcenter-rot",
        grid_width = unit(0.5, "in"),
        legend_height = unit(6, "in")))

draw(heatmap, 
     heatmap_legend_side = "right", 
     annotation_legend_side = "right", 
     annotation_legend_list = list(cluster_legend, dataset_legend))

dev.off()
```

![With stricter filtering criteria, it appears the dataset batch effect has gotten a lot better. However, further works needs to be done](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240626/filtered_ubc_data_heatmap.png)
